channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇪🇺 Europe Seeks Role in Iran War but Remains on Sidelines
item.description: A British-French plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz would give Europe a role in the conflict, but Tehran and Washington continue to call the shots, leaving European powers struggling for influence.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Mideast Crises Divide Europe Over Fuel Costs and Israel Policy
item.description: European diplomats meeting in Luxembourg are grappling with rising fuel costs from the Iran conflict and policy divisions over Israel, as the war in Ukraine and Middle East tensions strain EU unity.

item.title: 🇨🇾 Cyprus President Urges EU to Create Defense Playbook for Member States Under Attack
item.description: Cyprus President Nikos Christodoulides says EU leaders must prepare a playbook for responding when member states call for help during attacks, as the bloc faces growing security challenges from multiple fronts.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Trump Cuba Threats Revive Exile Hopes and Fears Over Property Claims
item.description: President Donald Trump's threat of military intervention in Cuba is raising hopes among Cuban Americans for regime change, while reigniting complex debates over property claims seized after the Castro revolution.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Uncertainty Hangs Over US-Iran Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: President Trump says he does not want to extend the ceasefire with Iran as the clock ticks down on the deadline, with diplomatic efforts to end the seven-week war facing significant obstacles.

item.title: 🌍 World's largest condom maker Karex to raise prices up to 30% due to Iran war
item.description: Malaysian condom manufacturer Karex, which produces one in five condoms globally, announced price increases of up to 30% citing supply chain disruptions from the Iran conflict and rising raw material costs.

item.title: 🗳️ Voting rights groups sue Trump administration over state voter roll demands
item.description: Multiple civil rights organizations have filed lawsuits challenging the Trump administration's demands for state voter registration data, arguing it exceeds federal authority.

item.title: 🔍 Florida launches criminal probe into OpenAI over deadly university shooting
item.description: Florida authorities have opened a criminal investigation into OpenAI regarding ChatGPT's alleged role in a fatal shooting at a university campus.

item.title: 🗳️ Virginia voters approve Democratic-backed congressional map
item.description: Virginia voters approved a new congressional redistricting map backed by Democrats, reshaping the state's electoral landscape ahead of the midterm elections.

item.title: 🇬🇧 New testimony raises pressure on UK PM Starmer over US envoy appointment
item.description: Fresh testimony has intensified scrutiny on UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office over the controversial appointment of Peter Mandelson as US ambassador.

item.title: 🛡️ Denmark Chooses European SAMP/T Over US Patriot for Air Defense
item.description: Denmark selected the European SAMP/T air defense system over the US Patriot, marking a shift toward European defense procurement autonomy amid transatlantic tensions.

item.title: ⚔️ Trump Says Iran Keeping Hormuz Closed to Save Face
item.description: President Trump claimed Iran is maintaining its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to preserve national dignity, as diplomatic efforts to end the conflict continue.

item.title: ⛪ Israel Jails Soldiers for Vandalizing Jesus Statue in Lebanon
item.description: Israel sentenced several soldiers to prison for vandalizing a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon, drawing condemnation from Christian communities worldwide.

item.title: 🤖 Germany Faces EU Resistance Over AI Deregulation Push
item.description: Germany's push for lighter AI regulations faces growing opposition in Brussels as EU member states debate the balance between innovation and safety.

item.title: ⚡ EU Launches Emergency Energy Plan to Counter Iran Crisis
item.description: The European Union unveiled an emergency energy strategy to mitigate supply disruptions from the Iran conflict, seeking alternative energy sources.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President Cancels Africa Trip After China Pressures Overflight Denials
item.description: Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te cancelled a planned visit to Eswatini after China pressured African countries to deny overflight permissions, escalating cross-strait tensions.

item.title: 📈 Global Stocks Hit Record Highs Despite Ongoing Iran Conflict
item.description: Global stock markets reached new highs as investor optimism about AI technology offset concerns about the Iran conflict and energy prices.

item.title: 🇯🇵 China Intensifies Targeted Pressure on Japan Amid Taiwan Strait Tensions
item.description: China has stepped up diplomatic pressure on Japan through targeted measures as regional tensions over Taiwan and maritime disputes continue to escalate.

item.title: 💼 US Trade Rep Tells Mexican Companies Tariffs Will Stay
item.description: The US Trade Representative told Mexican business leaders that Trump-era tariffs will remain in place, dampening hopes for near-term trade relief.

item.title: 🇨🇳 With Tariffs Stalled, Trump China Policy Drifts
item.description: President Trump's China policy has entered a period of uncertainty as tariff negotiations stall and administration officials struggle to define a clear strategy.

item.title: ⚔️ Trump Says He Does Not Want to Extend Iran Ceasefire
item.description: President Trump stated he does not want to extend the ceasefire with Iran, raising questions about the next phase of the conflict as the deadline approaches.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US to Indefinitely Extend Iran Ceasefire, Unclear If Iran Agrees
item.description: The United States announced an indefinite extension of the ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request, though Iran's agreement to the extension remains unclear.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Pushes Military Independence in Arctic Amid US Tensions
item.description: Canada's military is demonstrating its ability to operate independently in the Arctic as tensions with the United States over sovereignty and resource rights intensify.

item.title: 🇳🇱 Europe Must Take Greater Responsibility for Own Security, Dutch Intelligence Warns
item.description: The Dutch intelligence agency warned that Europe must take greater responsibility for its own security as global threats mount and US attention shifts elsewhere.

item.title: 🛢️ Middle East Scrambles for Alternative Oil Export Routes as Hormuz Remains Blocked
item.description: Middle Eastern oil producers are seeking alternative export routes as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, disrupting global energy supplies.
